title: Module 0 Project: New Inventory Intake
summary: Add a five-item approved inventory batch through five cumulative INSERT steps.

sketchBlocks.title: New Inventory Intake Project
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will take on the role of a Junior Data Operations Analyst tasked with adding a batch of new inventory items to the existing database. The goal is to insert these items without altering any existing records, ensuring that each new entry is deliberate and precise. You'll use explicit column lists for each INSERT statement, apply SQL NULL and DEFAULT values where appropriate, and verify that all pre-existing rows remain unchanged. This project will help you master the skills needed to manage data insertion tasks effectively.

quizDraft.title: Add the First Approved Item
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new item, 'Desk Organizer', into the inventory with explicit columns and values.
quizDraft.hint: Use an explicit column list to insert the Desk Organizer with all required details.

help.concept: Explicit column lists ensure that each value is inserted into the correct column, preventing errors due to column order changes.
help.hint_1: List all columns explicitly in the INSERT statement.
help.hint_2: Ensure the 'Desk Organizer' has a unique id and all specified attributes.

quizDraft.title: Record an Item with No Notes
quizDraft.prompt: Add 'Cable Labels' to the inventory, ensuring the notes column is set to SQL NULL.
quizDraft.hint: Use SQL NULL for the notes column to indicate no notes are available.

help.concept: SQL NULL represents a missing or undefined value, distinct from an empty string or zero.
help.hint_1: Include SQL NULL in the VALUES list for the notes column.
help.hint_2: Ensure the 'Cable Labels' entry uses a unique id and correct attributes.

quizDraft.title: Use the Approved Defaults
quizDraft.prompt: Insert 'Shipping Pouch' into the inventory, omitting status and last_updated to use defaults.
quizDraft.hint: Omit the status and last_updated columns to use their default values.

help.concept: Omitting columns in an INSERT statement allows SQLite to use their declared default values.
help.hint_1: Do not include status and last_updated in the column list.
help.hint_2: Ensure 'Shipping Pouch' has a unique id and correct attributes.

quizDraft.title: Add Two Items as One Batch
quizDraft.prompt: Insert 'Packing Tape' and 'Label Roll' in one multi-row INSERT statement.
quizDraft.hint: Use a multi-row INSERT to add both items with the same column list.

help.concept: Multi-row INSERT allows adding multiple records in a single statement, improving efficiency.
help.hint_1: Include both items in the VALUES section of the INSERT statement.
help.hint_2: Ensure each item has a unique id and correct attributes.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Insert the fourth and fifth approved items
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  (104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
  (105, 'Label Roll', 'Office', 1.99, 'active', 0, 'Easy to peel', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);
quizDraft.solutionCode:
-- Insert the fourth and fifth approved items
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  (104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
  (105, 'Label Roll', 'Office', 1.99, 'active', 0, 'Easy to peel', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);

solutionFiles.content:
-- Insert the fourth and fifth approved items
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  (104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
  (105, 'Label Roll', 'Office', 1.99, 'active', 0, 'Easy to peel', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);

quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);

quizDraft.title: Finish the Intake Handoff
quizDraft.prompt: Ensure all five new items are inserted correctly and verify the entire inventory remains consistent.
quizDraft.hint: Verify that all new items are present and existing items are unchanged.

help.concept: Verification ensures that the intended changes are applied correctly without affecting existing data.
help.hint_1: Use a SELECT statement to verify all new and existing items.
help.hint_2: Check that the ids and attributes of all items match expectations.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Verify all new items and existing inventory
SELECT * FROM inventory_items ORDER BY id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
-- Verify all new items and existing inventory
SELECT * FROM inventory_items ORDER BY id;
